req.title: Delete patient (soft) (boundary)
req.statement: A system-admin shall soft-delete a patient resource; deletion shall not remove history. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-HLT-029 mandates: (1) admin can soft-delete a Patient via DELETE /Patient/{id}, (2) the resource is marked deleted but history is preserved via _history, (3) non-admins receive 403. Four tests cover these concerns: EP positive (admin deletes, resource marked deleted), NEG (non-admin gets 403), BVA on timestamp boundary (lastUpdated at Unix epoch boundary), and STC tracing the active→deleted→history-retrievable state chain.

tests.name: test_admin_soft_delete_patient_marks_resource_deleted
preconditions: A Patient resource with a known ID exists and is active
actions: POST /Patient to create a patient and capture {id}
expected: GET /Patient/{id} returns 410 Gone or resource with active=false / meta.tag 'deleted'
GET /Patient/{id}/_history returns bundle containing at least 2 entries (created + deleted)
trace: R-HLT-029: deletion shall not remove history
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_admin_soft_delete_patient_marks_resource_deleted(
    admin_headers=<fixture:admin_headers>,
    new_patient_id=<helper_fn:create_patient(admin_headers)>
):
    patient_id = new_patient_id

    del_resp = requests.delete(f"{BASE}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 204)

    get_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code in (200, 410)
    if get_resp.status_code == 200:
        body = get_resp.json()
        tags = [t.get("code") for t in body.get("meta", {}).get("tag", [])]
        assert body.get("active") is False or "deleted" in tags

    hist_resp = requests.get(f"{BASE}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history", headers=admin_headers)
    assert hist_resp.status_code == 200
    bundle = hist_resp.json()
    assert bundle.get("resourceType") == "Bundle"
    assert len(bundle.get("entry", [])) >= 2

tests.name: test_non_admin_soft_delete_patient_returns_403
preconditions: A Patient resource with a known ID exists and is active
actions: POST /Patient with admin credentials to create patient and capture {id}
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with severity 'error'
trace: R-HLT-029: error pathway – 403 when not admin
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_non_admin_soft_delete_patient_returns_403(
    admin_headers=<fixture:admin_headers>,
    practitioner_headers=<fixture:practitioner_headers>,
    new_patient_id=<helper_fn:create_patient(admin_headers)>
):
    patient_id = new_patient_id

    del_resp = requests.delete(
        f"{BASE}/Patient/{patient_id}",
        headers=practitioner_headers
    )
    assert del_resp.status_code == 403

    body = del_resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "OperationOutcome"
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    severities = [i.get("severity") for i in issues]
    assert "error" in severities or "fatal" in severities

tests.name: test_soft_delete_patient_history_timestamp_at_unix_epoch_boundary
preconditions: A Patient resource is created with birthDate set to '1970-01-01' (Unix epoch boundary)
The resource's meta.lastUpdated can be inspected from _history
actions: Inspect lastUpdated timestamp of the deletion entry and birthDate of the original entry
expected: Deleted entry meta.lastUpdated is a valid ISO-8601 datetime and NOT null
Original entry preserves birthDate='1970-01-01' without truncation or overflow
trace: R-HLT-029: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
R-HLT-029: deletion shall not remove history
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timezone

BASE = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"
EPOCH_DATE = "1970-01-01"

def test_soft_delete_patient_history_timestamp_at_unix_epoch_boundary(
    admin_headers=<fixture:admin_headers>
):
    payload = {"resourceType": "Patient", "birthDate": EPOCH_DATE}
    create = requests.post(f"{BASE}/Patient", json=payload, headers=admin_headers)
    assert create.status_code in (200, 201)
    patient_id = create.json()["id"]

    del_resp = requests.delete(f"{BASE}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 204)

    hist = requests.get(f"{BASE}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history", headers=admin_headers)
    assert hist.status_code == 200
    entries = hist.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 2

    del_entry = entries[0]["resource"]
    last_updated = del_entry.get("meta", {}).get("lastUpdated")
    assert last_updated is not None
    parsed = datetime.fromisoformat(last_updated.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
    assert parsed > datetime(1970, 1, 1, tzinfo=timezone.utc)

    orig_entry = entries[-1]["resource"]
    assert orig_entry.get("birthDate") == EPOCH_DATE

tests.name: test_soft_delete_patient_state_transition_active_to_deleted_to_history
preconditions: A Patient resource exists in state: active
actions: Verify patient is no longer active (state: deleted)
Verify version count incremented in history (state: history-retrievable)
expected: Initial GET returns 200 with patient data
DELETE transitions resource to deleted state (200/204)
Post-delete GET returns 410 or resource flagged deleted
Latest _history entry versionId > first entry versionId
trace: R-HLT-029: resource marked deleted; retrievable via _history (expected outcomes)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE = "http://localhost:8080/fhir"

def test_soft_delete_patient_state_transition_active_to_deleted_to_history(
    admin_headers=<fixture:admin_headers>,
    new_patient_id=<helper_fn:create_patient(admin_headers)>
):
    patient_id = new_patient_id

    # State: active
    get1 = requests.get(f"{BASE}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert get1.status_code == 200
    v_before = int(get1.json().get("meta", {}).get("versionId", 1))

    # Transition: active → deleted
    del_resp = requests.delete(f"{BASE}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 204)

    # State: deleted
    get2 = requests.get(f"{BASE}/Patient/{patient_id}", headers=admin_headers)
    assert get2.status_code in (200, 410)
    if get2.status_code == 200:
        body2 = get2.json()
        tags = [t.get("code") for t in body2.get("meta", {}).get("tag", [])]
        assert body2.get("active") is False or "deleted" in tags

    # State: history-retrievable
    hist = requests.get(f"{BASE}/Patient/{patient_id}/_history", headers=admin_headers)
    assert hist.status_code == 200
    entries = hist.json().get("entry", [])
    assert len(entries) >= 2
    v_after = int(entries[0]["resource"].get("meta", {}).get("versionId", 2))
    assert v_after > v_before
